item.description: Fan Milk PLC began the 2026 financial year with a robust performance, reporting a profit before tax of GH₵61.2 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, marking an 84% increase compared to GH₵33.2 million in the same period last year. Revenue grew by 33% to GH₵321.6 million, driven by steady consumer demand and resilient distribution networks despite economic pressures. Gross profit rose significantly from GH₵90.7 million to GH₵150.3 million, reflecting improved cost management, although the cost of sales also increased. Operating expenses, including sales, distribution, and administrative costs, saw notable rises, while finance costs nearly doubled due to higher interest obligations. Income tax expense surged to GH₵32 million, reducing the profit after tax to GH₵27.6 million, a 14.6% increase from the prior year. Earnings per share improved from GH₵0.21 to GH₵0.24. The company invested GH₵3.4 million in capital expenditure and retained earnings instead of paying dividends. C

item.title: Artist KC Green accuses AI startup Artisan of unauthorized use of his ‘This is fine’ comic in advertising
item.description:
Cartoonist KC Green, best known for creating the widely recognized “This is fine” comic, has accused AI startup Artisan of using his artwork without permission in a recent advertising campaign. The original comic, first published in 2013 as part of Green’s webcomic “Gunshow,” features a dog calmly sitting in a burning room and has since become a long-lasting internet meme. According to posts circulating on social media, Artisan allegedly modified the image for a subway advertisement, changing the caption to reference a failing business pipeline while promoting its AI sales tool, “Ava the AI BDR.”
Green publicly stated that he did not authorize the use of his work and described the situation as theft, comparing it to broader concerns about how AI companies use creative content. He also expressed frustration that he may need to pursue legal action, noting that doing so would take time away from his creative work. Artisan responded by saying it respects Green and has reached out to him,

item.title: The Truth Behind Intermittent Fasting: Benefits, Risks, and Who It Works For
item.description: Intermittent fasting (IF) has gained popularity as a health trend, but its effectiveness varies from person to person. Unlike traditional diets that focus on what you eat, IF is all about when you eat. By alternating between eating and fasting windows, it encourages the body to shift from burning glucose to burning fat for energy. Although IF can lead to weight loss and improve metabolic health, it is not a miracle solution or superior to other dietary strategies. The 16:8 method, 5:2 diet, and alternate-day fasting are common approaches, but they are not without their challenges, including hunger, irritability, and the risk of overeating during eating windows. While some studies suggest benefits for weight loss, diabetes management, and cardiovascular health, the long-term effects are still unclear. Furthermore, IF is not suitable for everyone—pregnant women, children, and those with eating disorders should avoid it. Ultimately, IF is just one tool in a broader healthy lifestyle that

item.title: Ed Sheeran joins Ipswich Town team to celebrate promotion to Premier League
item.description: British singer-songwriter Ed Sheeran, who grew up in Ipswich, marked the football club Ipswich Town's promotion to the Premier League by joining the team in their dressing room for a celebratory singalong. Known for his strong ties to his hometown, Sheeran shared the moment with players and staff, adding a personal and joyful touch to the occasion. The celebration highlighted the emotional significance of the promotion for both the team and the local community. Ipswich Town, historically a well-known club in English football, had been working towards this achievement for several years. Fans and local residents expressed their excitement on social media as Sheeran's presence amplified the sense of pride and accomplishment surrounding the club's return to top-flight football. This event serves as a reminder of the deep connection between local celebrities and their hometown communities, and how such moments can inspire both players and supporters alike.

item.title: Former NYC Mayor Rudy Giuliani Hospitalized in Critical but Stable Condition
item.description: Former New York City Mayor Rudy Giuliani has been admitted to the hospital and is currently in critical but stable condition, according to statements posted on X by political strategist Ted Goodman. Goodman, who co-hosted a livestream program with Giuliani, described the former mayor as a resilient fighter, emphasizing his determination and strength in facing challenges. Supporters and the public are being encouraged to keep Giuliani in their thoughts and prayers during this time. The hospitalization follows a recent incident in New Hampshire where Giuliani was injured in a car crash while assisting a domestic violence victim, highlighting his ongoing involvement in public service. No additional details about his medical condition or the cause of hospitalization have been disclosed. The news has prompted widespread concern, reflecting Giuliani's prominence as a political figure and former mayor. Media coverage continues to follow his condition closely, with updates expected as more inf

item.title: WHO investigates hantavirus cases linked to cruise ship voyage in the Atlantic
item.description:
A suspected hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship traveling in the Atlantic Ocean has resulted in three deaths and several serious illnesses, according to the World Health Organization (WHO). The situation is currently under investigation, with at least one confirmed case of the virus. Health authorities are working to determine the extent of the outbreak and to identify how the infection spread among passengers.
One of the infected individuals is receiving intensive care treatment in a South African hospital, while efforts are underway to evacuate two additional symptomatic passengers from the vessel for medical care. The WHO stated that detailed laboratory and epidemiological investigations are ongoing, including genetic sequencing of the virus. Medical teams are also providing care and monitoring to passengers and crew still onboard.
Hantavirus is typically transmitted through exposure to rodent urine, droppings, or saliva, though rare cases of person-to-person transmission hav

item.title: Researchers Investigate Natural Sugar 2dDR for Potential Hair Regrowth
item.description: Male pattern baldness affects roughly half of all men, yet only two FDA-approved treatments currently exist. Recent research from the University of Sheffield and COMSATS University Pakistan suggests that a naturally occurring sugar, 2-deoxy-D-ribose (2dDR), may promote hair regrowth. Initially studied for its role in wound healing in mice, 2dDR was found to enhance blood vessel formation, which unexpectedly also stimulated hair growth around healing areas. Follow-up experiments modeled testosterone-driven hair loss in mice to mimic human male pattern baldness. Small doses of 2dDR improved several hair health indicators, including follicle density, hair thickness, and melanin coverage, showing effectiveness comparable to minoxidil, a commonly used topical treatment. Researchers note that 2dDR is naturally occurring, inexpensive, and stable, making it a promising candidate for further study. While findings remain in early stages and have not yet been tested in humans, the approach target

item.title: An Oakland Party Experiments with Equalizing Guests’ Heights Using Custom Platform Shoes
item.description: In Oakland, artist Lucian Novosel hosted a unique social experiment known as a 'same-height party,' inspired by German artist Hans Hemmert's 1997 installation 'Level.' The event aimed to equalize all participants’ heights using custom-made platform shoes, allowing guests of different statures to meet eye-to-eye. Guests ranged from 4 feet 11 inches to 6 feet 5 inches, and Novosel used 3D printing and foam to create stable, pyramid-shaped platforms for safety and balance. Walking sticks helped participants navigate the height adjustments, and attendees tested their new perspectives through games, social interaction, and daily activities like grabbing objects or playing pool. Participants reported a shift in confidence and social ease, with taller guests feeling less conspicuous and shorter guests enjoying a new vantage point. The experience also became a conversation starter, breaking social barriers and encouraging empathy through shared perspective. After the event, the shoes were dona

item.title: Investigation Finds FDA Raised Concerns About COVID Vaccine Side Effects That Were Not Fully Addressed
item.description: A recent report by Senator Ron Johnson suggests that the Biden administration may have neglected potential COVID-19 vaccine side effects during the early rollout of the vaccines. The investigation focused on Dr. Ana Szarfman, a senior FDA official and data analyst, who identified dozens of statistically significant safety signals using advanced techniques in the Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS). These signals included serious conditions such as sudden cardiac death, Bell’s palsy, and pulmonary infarction. Despite sharing these findings with FDA officials responsible for vaccine safety, Dr. Szarfman was reportedly told to halt further analysis. Experts at the FDA expressed concern that highlighting these signals could fuel anti-vaccine sentiment. The report criticizes the administration’s focus on promoting universal vaccine uptake, especially among younger populations with low risk from COVID-19, over thoroughly investigating potential safety issues. While VAERS is a repo

item.title: NASA Explores Using Drones to Study Martian Volcanic Caves for Signs of Life
item.description: Orpheus is a proposed NASA mission that aims to explore volcanic fissures and caves in the Cerberus Fossae region of Mars using advanced drones. Following the success of Ingenuity, the first helicopter to fly on Mars, NASA is investigating the potential of larger drones to access areas that traditional rovers cannot. Cerberus Fossae, located near the Martian equator, features young lava flows, numerous caves, and depressions that may harbor conditions suitable for microbial life due to the combination of underground ice, geothermal heat, and radiation protection. Orpheus would target five aligned depressions and caves near the Cerberus Tholus 1 volcanic cone, which are difficult to explore with standard rovers but ideal for drones. The mission aims to search for bio-signatures, study the origin and evolution of Mars, and gather data on its water history. Named after the mythological Orpheus who tamed Cerberus with music, the mission is currently a concept led by Connor Bunn and Pascal

item.title: One dead and two injured in early morning shooting outside Florence-Firestone strip mall
item.description:
A shooting early Sunday morning in the unincorporated Florence-Firestone area of Los Angeles County left one man dead and two others injured, according to authorities. Deputies from the Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department responded to reports of gunfire at approximately 1:57 a.m. in the 1600 block of Firestone Boulevard. Upon arrival, they discovered two individuals suffering from gunshot wounds in the parking lot of a strip mall. A third victim, identified as a security guard working at a nearby location, was struck by a stray bullet and was not believed to have been an intended target.
The deceased victim, a 35-year-old man, was pronounced dead at the scene. The two surviving victims, aged 25 and 27, were transported to a hospital for treatment. Authorities reported that one of the injured individuals was in stable condition, while the other remained in critical condition. Officials did not specify which of the victims was the security guard.
Investigators believe the incident

item.title: Ask.com and Jeeves Shut Down After 30 Years of Service
item.description: After almost 30 years, Ask.com, once a pioneer in search engines, has officially shut down, marking the end of an era for its famous mascot, Jeeves. The company, which began as Ask Jeeves in 1996, was designed to answer questions in natural language, mimicking the persona of a clever English butler. Despite its early success, Ask Jeeves struggled to maintain relevance against competitors like Google and Yahoo. After being bought by InterActiveCorp in 2005, it was rebranded as Ask.com and attempted to stay competitive by introducing features like hyperlocal search maps. However, it couldn't match the dominance of Google or newer platforms like Quora. The closure of Ask.com is part of a broader trend, as other once-popular services like AOL dial-up and AIM also fade into history. The death of Ask.com is a reminder of how quickly the internet evolves, and how even the most innovative platforms can become obsolete as technology progresses.

item.title: AI Innovations Aim to Improve Diagnosis and Treatment of Drug-Resistant Infections
item.description: Antibiotic resistance is an escalating global health crisis, responsible for over a million deaths annually and contributing to millions more. Traditional treatment often relies on physicians' judgment, with diagnostic tests taking two to three days—time that can be critical, especially in cases like sepsis, where every hour of delay increases mortality risk by 4 to 9 percent. Ara Darzi, director of the Institute of Global Health Innovation at Imperial College London, highlighted at WIRED Health 2026 that AI-powered diagnostics now offer over 99% accuracy without additional lab infrastructure. These tools are particularly valuable for rural and underserved regions. AI can also accelerate drug discovery and predict bacterial resistance patterns, demonstrated by a collaboration between the UK’s NHS and Google DeepMind, which solved a decade-old resistance problem in just 48 hours. Despite technological advances, pharmaceutical companies have largely abandoned antibiotic development due t

item.title: Texas Startup Uses Robots to Construct Homes from Local Clay
item.description: A startup located south of Austin, Texas, is pioneering a novel approach to home construction by using robots to build houses from clay sourced directly on-site. The construction process begins with gathering local soil, which is then mixed and prepared on a build plate. Robots equipped with claw-like tools pick up the clay, transport it to the walls, and deposit it carefully. After placing the material, the same robots switch to a hammer attachment to compact and shape the walls, simulating traditional adobe building techniques. Machine learning algorithms continuously monitor and adjust the building process to ensure smooth, solid walls. The project is taking place at Proto-Town, a testing ranch between Lockhart and Luling that hosts experimental technologies, ranging from anti-drone systems to nuclear reactors. The startup plans to construct its next home on this property and aims to build more than 20 homes over the coming year. This approach aims to make sustainable, low-cost home

item.title: Merz Stresses Need for Strong US-Germany Ties Despite Differences with Trump
item.description: In a recent interview, German political leader Friedrich Merz emphasized the significance of the transatlantic relationship between Germany and the United States, especially in light of past disagreements with former U.S. President Donald Trump. Merz, who serves as the leader of the Christian Democratic Union (CDU) in Germany, called for a continued commitment to NATO and the broader Western alliance, despite the tension that has marked Germany’s relationship with Trump during his presidency. While expressing concern over some of Trump's policies, Merz stressed that the overarching goal should be the preservation of strong, cooperative ties between the U.S. and Germany for the benefit of both countries and international security. The CDU leader's remarks come at a time when Europe is grappling with complex global challenges, including rising tensions with Russia and the evolving role of NATO. Merz's position highlights Germany’s ongoing balancing act as it seeks to maintain strong rela

item.title: Authorities Confirm WHCD Shooting Suspect Fired Weapon in Attempted Assassination of President
item.description: Jeanine Pirro, U.S. attorney for the District of Columbia, confirmed that the suspect involved in the White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting definitively fired at a Secret Service agent. Evidence includes video footage of the suspect shooting, testimony from the agent, and forensic analysis linking pellets from the suspect’s Mossberg pump-action shotgun to the agent’s vest. The suspect is charged with attempted assassination of President Donald Trump, along with firearm-related offenses. While the suspect’s manifesto does not explicitly name Trump, Pirro stated that evidence indicates Trump was the target. Authorities emphasized the suspect’s premeditated intent and his calculated plan to harm anyone in his path. Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche confirmed that the suspect fired a single shot. Pirro described the suspect as intelligent, holding a master’s degree and having worked at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory, asserting that he is not insane. President Trump has publicly

item.title: Search Underway for Two U.S. Soldiers Missing After Exercise in Morocco
item.description:
Two U.S. Army soldiers have gone missing in southwestern Morocco following their participation in the African Lion military exercise, a large multinational training operation involving more than 30 countries. According to U.S. Africa Command (AFRICOM), the soldiers disappeared Saturday evening near the Cap Draa Training Area, close to the Atlantic coast. A U.S. defense official stated that the soldiers were not engaged in active training at the time but had gone on a recreational hike after the day’s exercises concluded.
When the soldiers failed to return as expected, U.S. and Moroccan forces immediately initiated a joint search and rescue operation. The effort includes helicopters, naval vessels, mountain rescue teams, and divers, reflecting the challenging terrain of the area, which consists of mountainous landscapes, desert, and semi-desert plains near ocean cliffs.
The African Lion exercise, which began in April and is scheduled to conclude in early May, spans multiple countries

item.title: Fatal Hit-and-Run Collision in Playa Del Rey Kills Two, Including a 1-Year-Old
item.description: Two people, including a 1-year-old child, were killed in a tragic hit-and-run crash on Sunday morning in Playa Del Rey, California. The collision occurred around 4:34 a.m. near the intersection of Culver Boulevard and Vista Del Mar. The accident involved a southbound vehicle that crossed into the northbound lane and collided with an oncoming car. Authorities reported that one man died at the scene, while a 1-year-old child was rushed to the hospital but later succumbed to their injuries. Another woman was critically injured and transported to the hospital, while a second man, whose condition is still unclear, was also hospitalized. The driver of the vehicle responsible for the crash, described as a white Jeep, fled the scene heading north on Vista Del Mar and remains at large. The Los Angeles Police Department has launched an investigation into the incident, though no further details about the victims' identities or their relationships have been disclosed.

item.title: Report Details U.S. Intelligence Activities Targeting the Vatican
item.description: A recent report by investigative journalist Ken Klippenstein claims that the U.S. government has been monitoring the Vatican and Pope Leo XIV through a multi-agency intelligence effort. According to sources cited by Klippenstein, this surveillance intensified following critical remarks by former President Donald Trump about the Pope’s foreign policy and stance on crime. The report details involvement from the CIA, NSA, FBI, and the State Department, including human intelligence operatives within the Vatican, monitoring of communications, and the production of daily intelligence assessments focused on Vatican affairs. While much of the collaboration between the U.S. and Vatican is portrayed as legitimate, such as coordination on cybersecurity, human trafficking, and art theft, Klippenstein suggests it also provides cover for intelligence-gathering. The report notes specialized U.S. military capabilities, including a Vatican-specific language code for Ecclesiastical Latin. These revelati

item.title: Secret Service Agent Wounded During White House Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting
item.description: During the White House Correspondents’ Dinner, a Secret Service agent was wounded when a suspected gunman, identified as Cole Tomas Allen, fired a shotgun. According to US Attorney Jeanine Pirro, forensic evidence confirms that a pellet from Allen’s Mossberg pump-action shotgun struck the agent’s vest. Hotel surveillance video and ballroom audio indicate that six shots were fired in total: one by Allen and five by responding officers. Additional footage is expected to be released. Before bypassing security, Allen entered a room to remove a long coat concealing the shotgun. A bomb detection dog inspected the area, but it was not trained to detect gunpowder from ammunition. Allen faces initial charges of discharging a firearm during a violent crime, with potential for additional charges as the grand jury investigation progresses. Pirro emphasized that, despite an alleged manifesto not naming President Trump explicitly, evidence shows Trump was the intended target, based on Allen’s action

item.title: Analysis of the King's Influence on Trump's Popularity
item.description: The content of this news article could not be accessed due to server restrictions, so a full summary cannot be provided. Based on the title, the article likely discusses whether a prominent figure, referred to as 'the King,' has had any measurable impact on former President Donald Trump's political standing, public perception, or campaign momentum. The piece might explore factors such as public endorsements, media coverage, or recent political events that could influence Trump's influence or approval ratings. Since the article text was inaccessible, the summary remains speculative, focusing on the probable context and themes suggested by the title.

item.title: Reid Hoffman Advocates Using AI as a Second Opinion in Healthcare
item.description: Reid Hoffman, cofounder of LinkedIn and investor in major tech companies, is now focusing on healthcare through his startup, Manas AI. The company aims to accelerate drug discovery for cancers and rare diseases, reducing the typical decade-long process to just a few years. Hoffman advocates for using advanced AI models as a 'second opinion' in medical practice, arguing that not doing so could be considered a medical oversight. He emphasizes that AI, despite not being specifically trained in medicine, has processed vast amounts of information and can complement doctors' expertise. Hoffman uses AI himself for personal health decisions and encourages doctors to do the same. He also sees potential for AI to support healthcare systems under strain, like the NHS, by serving as a medical assistant for early triage and regulatory guidance. While Manas AI still relies on human judgment to filter AI-generated drug targets, Hoffman believes the technology could significantly expand the discovery

item.title: Trump Denies Iran Comment Despite On-Camera Evidence
item.description: On May 2, 2026, President Donald Trump addressed reporters at Palm Beach International Airport, responding to a question about a new Iranian peace proposal. When reminded of a statement he had made the previous night suggesting the U.S. might be better off not making a deal with Iran, Trump denied saying it, asserting that his comments were about the time it would take Iran to rebuild if the U.S. left. However, video evidence from a speech he gave on May 1 at the Forum Club of the Palm Beaches clearly shows Trump making the very remark he denied. This incident is part of a broader pattern of Trump denying or mischaracterizing statements he has made publicly, including comments about releasing military footage in 2025, his 2024 campaign promises regarding the war in Ukraine, and repeated denials of statements about political opponents in previous years. Fact-checking shows that such denials often conflict with on-camera evidence, raising concerns about consistency and accuracy in his pu

item.title: Premier Inn plans significant job reductions in UK and Ireland amid cost pressures
item.description: Premier Inn's parent company has announced plans to reduce its workforce in the UK and Ireland by over 12%, citing financial pressures and a need to streamline operations. The decision comes as the hospitality industry continues to face challenges including rising operational costs, shifts in consumer behavior, and lingering effects of the COVID-19 pandemic. Management stated that the cuts are part of a broader strategy to improve efficiency and ensure long-term sustainability for the company. Employees affected by the layoffs will receive support packages, and the company emphasized that the reductions, while difficult, are necessary to maintain the business's stability. This move reflects broader trends in the hotel and travel sector, where companies are balancing cost management with efforts to adapt to changing market conditions. Analysts suggest that while the job cuts are significant, they may help the company strengthen its position in a competitive market.

item.title: How to Choose a Long-Lasting Luxury Handbag
item.description: The article explores what truly makes a luxury handbag a worthwhile investment. It argues that an investment bag should not be bought with the hope of resale profit but rather for its durability and cost per wear over time. Using clear examples, the article compares expensive bags that last years versus cheaper bags that wear out quickly, showing that the initial cost is not the sole measure of value. It outlines four key markers of a well-made bag: the type of leather, the quality of hardware, the lining, and the edge finish. Smooth leather and hand-woven leather differ significantly in longevity, while sturdy hardware and carefully chosen linings signal attention to construction quality. A hand-painted edge is a quick way to judge craftsmanship. The article highlights three bags from the Moss & Fog collection — the Charlotte, the Paloma, and the Adria — each designed for different uses and styles, emphasizing that thoughtful design and durability define a true investment handbag. By

item.title: Secretary of State Marco Rubio Participates in Wedding DJ Event Amid Controversial Iran Conflict
item.description: On May 3, 2026, Secretary of State Marco Rubio appeared behind DJ equipment at a family wedding in an attempt to energize the crowd, according to a video shared by White House deputy chief of staff Dan Scavino. In the footage, Rubio mimics DJ movements and pumps his fist to the beat of the song “Shiver” by John Summit & Hayla, though he appears not to actually operate the equipment. The video circulated widely on social media, drawing criticism for its timing and tone, given the ongoing war with Iran led by President Donald Trump, which has seen massive public disapproval. The conflict, now over two months long and costing an estimated $25 billion, is creating economic strain, with gas prices averaging $4.44 per gallon and projected to rise further. While Rubio described Iranian negotiators as experienced in a recent Fox News interview, his lighthearted wedding performance contrasted sharply with the serious international crisis. Social media users expressed frustration, emphasizing th

item.title: Greg Abel’s Inaugural Berkshire Hathaway Shareholder Meeting: What to Expect
item.description: This weekend’s Berkshire Hathaway annual shareholder meeting marks the first time Greg Abel leads the event as CEO, signaling the start of a new era for the conglomerate. Warren Buffett remains chairman but will sit in the audience rather than at the center of the stage, underscoring the orderly succession plan Buffett has long emphasized. The gathering, held in Omaha and often dubbed the “Woodstock for Capitalists,” is expected once again to draw tens of thousands of shareholders and investors eager to hear management’s outlook on Berkshire’s businesses and portfolio. Attendees will have the opportunity to ask Abel and other executives questions, and CNBC’s Becky Quick will moderate a major portion of the Q&A session. The company will also release its quarterly results just before the Saturday morning meeting, giving investors a fresh look at operating earnings and capital‑allocation moves. As Berkshire adjusts to life after Buffett’s day‑to‑day leadership, shareholders are closely wa

item.title: Ex-Nintendo President Claims Amazon Requested Potentially Illegal Pricing Support
item.description: At a recent lecture at NYU, former Nintendo of America President Reggie Fils-Aimé revealed that Amazon once attempted to pressure Nintendo into offering exceptionally steep discounts on their products, which could have violated U.S. antitrust and price discrimination laws. Fils-Aimé explained that Amazon wanted to undercut all other retailers, including Walmart, by demanding financial support from Nintendo to offer the lowest market prices. Nintendo refused, prioritizing legal compliance and fairness toward its retail partners. At the time, the Wii and DS were Nintendo's top-selling hardware, making the request particularly high-stakes. The decision led Nintendo to temporarily stop selling products to Amazon, though relations eventually normalized, and Nintendo consoles, including the Switch 2, are now available on the platform. Experts commenting on the matter noted that discounts are legal, but coordinated efforts to manipulate prices across retailers could constitute price fixing un

item.title: Hiker Dies After Falling Into Ravine on Mt. Wilson Trail in Los Angeles County
item.description:
A male hiker died on Saturday after falling a significant distance into a ravine along a trail on Mt. Wilson in Los Angeles County, according to local authorities. The Los Angeles Sheriff’s Department responded to the emergency shortly before noon after receiving reports of the سقوط. The incident took place near Mt. Wilson Road and the Little Santa Anita fire break, an area known for its steep and rugged terrain.
Emergency responders included deputies from the sheriff’s Temple Station, the Air 5 rescue helicopter, and members of the Sierra Madre Search & Rescue team. Firefighters also assisted in the operation. According to officials, the hiker was pulled from the ravine, and deputies attempted life-saving measures, including CPR. Despite these efforts, the man was pronounced dead at the scene.
Authorities have not yet determined whether the death was caused directly by injuries from the fall or if a prior medical issue contributed to the incident. The Los Angeles County coroner’s of

item.title: Second Missing Bangladeshi Student Found Dead in Tampa Bay
item.description: Authorities in Tampa Bay have confirmed the discovery of the body of a Bangladeshi student who had been reported missing, marking the second student in a related case to be found deceased. The body was located by a local kayaker, who immediately reported the finding to law enforcement officials. Investigators are currently conducting a thorough examination to determine the circumstances surrounding the student's death. This tragic event has raised concerns within both the local community and the Bangladeshi student community, highlighting issues related to student safety and the support networks available for international students. Officials have appealed for anyone with additional information to come forward as they continue their investigation. While the first missing student’s case is still under review, authorities are working to establish any potential connections between the two incidents. The university and local authorities have expressed condolences to the family and friends

item.title: Public skepticism grows as AI and crypto super PACs pour money into the 2026 midterms
item.description:
A new POLITICO poll shows that Americans remain wary of both artificial intelligence and cryptocurrency even as industry-backed super PACs pour huge sums into the 2026 midterm elections. The article says this creates a political risk for candidates who accept help from these groups, because the public may view the spending as another form of outsized special-interest influence.
The poll, conducted by Public First, found that many Americans think crypto is too risky and that AI is advancing too quickly. It also showed strong preference for traditional banks over crypto platforms, and broad support for tighter AI oversight. These attitudes suggest that the political advantages of big money may come with a voter backlash if the public starts associating candidates too closely with industry agendas.
The story highlights two major players: Leading the Future, a pro-AI super PAC that has raised more than $75 million, and Fairshake, a pro-crypto group that has already spent tens of millions

item.title: Understanding the Use and Benefits of Black Timber Cladding in UK Architecture
item.description: Black timber cladding, particularly charred timber inspired by the Japanese Shou Sugi Ban technique, has become increasingly popular in contemporary UK architecture. Unlike painted or coated timber, charred timber undergoes a transformation where the outer layer is converted to carbon, creating a hydrophobic, UV-resistant, and biologically stable surface. This process not only ensures minimal maintenance over decades but also produces a distinctive, textured aesthetic that evolves over time. There are different approaches to black timber cladding: deep charred timber offers the boldest appearance and lowest upkeep, brushed and stained charred timber provides a softer look while revealing the wood grain, and factory-coated ThermoWood provides a controlled black finish without charring. Black timber works best when applied strategically, such as on garden studios, building entrances, or primary elevations, balancing visual impact with scale. In rural and semi-rural contexts, it resonates

item.title: Weekly Highlights from Moss & Fog: Claymation, Deep Lakes, and Creative Design
item.description:
This weekly edition of Moss & Fog’s “Fog Signals” highlights a curated selection of stories focused on art, design, nature, and architecture. One of the central features explores the enduring appeal of claymation, emphasizing its tactile, handmade quality and the emotional depth that comes from visible human craftsmanship. Despite the availability of faster and more polished animation techniques, claymation continues to stand out for its texture and authenticity, offering a distinctive artistic experience.
The issue also dives into the natural world with a feature on the nine deepest lakes on Earth, showcasing the planet’s remarkable geological diversity and the awe-inspiring scale of these bodies of water. In the realm of illustration, the newsletter presents graphite drawings by artist Marín, who uses layered techniques to create depth and meaning, turning the medium itself into an active component of the artwork.
Design and architecture are also prominently featured. One article h

item.title: Subnautica 2 Pre-Launch Showcase Scheduled for May 9, 2026
item.description: The Subnautica development team has announced a Pre-Launch Showcase for Subnautica 2, scheduled for May 9, 2026, starting at 8:00 AM PDT. The event, which will run for approximately 120 minutes, will feature the debut of a new gameplay trailer, live conversations with developers, and game demos to provide fans with an early look at the upcoming game. The showcase will be broadcast across multiple platforms, including YouTube, Twitch, and Bilibili. Attendees can participate in a Twitch Drops event during the live stream, where watching for at least 20 minutes qualifies viewers to claim an in-game Seamoth Statue reward following the Early Access launch. Instructions for linking a Twitch account and redeeming rewards in-game are provided. In addition to the Twitch Drops, the developers will host a plushie giveaway, with participation details revealed during the live event and on official social media channels afterward. The showcase aims to engage the Subnautica community, build excitemen

item.title: A Survivor's Pursuit of Justice Against a Predatory Priest
item.description: Nancy Brier recounts her lifelong struggle with trauma after discovering that both she and her brother were sexually abused—she as a young girl and he by Father Sean. In her late twenties, she learned of her brother's abuse and his childhood plan to confront the priest with a gun, a plan he never carried out. Motivated to prevent further abuse, Nancy tracked Father Sean as he moved between parishes in Missouri, warning parents through letters and calls. Her investigation revealed a network of church officials protecting him and multiple other victims. She faced resistance from her own parish when trying to establish a support group for clergy abuse survivors. Over time, Nancy grappled with leaving the Catholic Church, the personal toll of her mission, and the lifelong impact of her early abuse. Despite other traumas, including cancer, wildfires, and the death of friends and family, she found resilience through advocacy, forgiveness, and standing up for others. The story illustrates the

item.title: Four Green Card Holders in New Jersey Face Federal Charges for Illegal Voting
item.description: Four green card holders living in New Jersey have been federally charged for illegally voting in U.S. elections and providing false statements on their applications for naturalization. The individuals—David Neewilly (73), Jacenth Beadle Exum (70), Idan Choresh (43), and Abhinandan Vig (33)—allegedly registered to vote under the false claim of U.S. citizenship. Neewilly is accused of voting in both the 2020 and 2024 general elections, Beadle Exum and Vig in 2020, and Choresh in 2022. Each also reportedly swore under penalty of perjury that they had never voted in a federal election when applying for citizenship. Federal law prohibits non-citizens from voting in federal elections and from making false statements to obtain U.S. citizenship, with penalties including fines, imprisonment, or both. Local exceptions exist in Washington D.C. and some municipalities in California, Vermont, and Maryland. Studies indicate non-citizen voting is rare but could potentially influence election outcomes

item.title: Minnesota Twins’ Starter Joe Ryan Exits Early Due to Injury
item.description: During the Minnesota Twins' game against the Toronto Blue Jays on Sunday, starting pitcher Joe Ryan had to leave the game after throwing just nine pitches. Ryan gave up a walk to Vladimir Guerrero Jr. before signaling to the dugout that he needed to exit. The team staff approached, but no physical examination was conducted; Ryan simply explained briefly, and the decision was made to pull him from the game. The nature of the injury remains unclear, though it appears to be related to a prior issue. This unexpected early exit puts additional pressure on the Minnesota bullpen, as they will need to cover the remaining innings. The Twins and their fans are now left hoping that the injury is minor and that Ryan will be able to return to the mound soon. The incident raises concerns about the health of one of the team's key starting pitchers and could impact the Twins’ performance in upcoming games.

item.title: Revisiting the Boltzmann Brain Paradox: Can Our Memories Be Random Illusions?
item.description: A new study revisits the unsettling Boltzmann brain hypothesis, which suggests that our memories, perceptions, and observations may not be based on a real past, but instead could arise from random entropy fluctuations. The research, conducted by David Wolpert, Carlo Rovelli, and Jordan Scharnhorst, examines a central paradox in physics: how time and entropy interact in shaping our sense of reality. According to the second law of thermodynamics, entropy tends to increase over time, leading to a direction of time, yet Boltzmann's H theorem suggests that entropy is time-symmetric, meaning it doesn't inherently favor a past or future. This raises a disconcerting possibility that our memories are not reliable records, but instead illusions created by chance. The study also uncovers subtle circular reasoning in many arguments related to time and entropy, offering a more transparent framework to understand these long-standing issues. The findings challenge our basic assumptions about memory,

item.title: OpenAI Adjusts ChatGPT After Unexpected Surge in ‘Goblin’ References
item.description:
According to reports highlighted by The Wall Street Journal, OpenAI recently modified ChatGPT’s internal instructions after discovering that newer versions of the model had begun frequently referencing goblins and similar creatures in unrelated conversations. Users, particularly programmers, noticed the unusual behavior when the chatbot used terms like “goblin” to describe coding issues or errors, sometimes repeatedly and without prompting. One user reported counting more than 20 such mentions during normal interactions.
The issue was traced back to a specific “nerdy” personality setting used in ChatGPT’s customization features. During training, this mode was rewarded for using playful and metaphorical language, especially involving fantastical creatures. As a result, the model began to overuse these metaphors. Although the “nerdy” style accounted for only a small percentage of responses (around 2.5%), it generated a disproportionately high share—over two-thirds—of all goblin-related

item.title: Analysis: Market Outlook for AbbVie Following Recent Earnings Performance
item.description: AbbVie recently reported impressive quarterly earnings that significantly surpassed Wall Street expectations, primarily fueled by the strong performance of its diverse immunology portfolio. Despite these positive financial results, the company's stock has faced ongoing scrutiny and investor apprehension. David Wainer, a senior contributor at The Wall Street Journal, analyzed the market's reaction, highlighting that while the immediate financial metrics are robust, structural concerns remain among institutional investors. These concerns are frequently tied to long-term revenue sustainability in key business segments, such as oncology and aesthetics, which face shifting competitive landscapes and potential regulatory pressures. Investors are also closely monitoring the company's drug pipeline and future guidance to gauge whether AbbVie can maintain its growth trajectory in the coming years. This dichotomy between strong current performance and future uncertainty creates a complex narrati

item.title: Hong Kong’s Historic Haw Par Mansion Set to Reopen as Cultural Center
item.description: Perched in Tai Hang, Hong Kong, the 91-year-old Haw Par Mansion is a rare remnant of the once sprawling Tiger Balm Garden estate built by the Aw family in the 1930s. Known for its distinctive Chinese Renaissance architectural style, the mansion and its front garden are now the only surviving structures from this historic site. Between 2019 and 2022, the building housed a music school founded by the Aw family. Following the school's closure, the government sought a new steward for the Grade 1 historic building. In 2025, the Foundation for Art and Culture (FAC), a non-profit organization, was selected to operate the mansion under a self-financing model. FAC plans to transform the site into Villa Haw Par, a dynamic cultural hub rather than a museum or theme park. Activities will include international artist residencies, film screenings, and a bookstore, creating a living cultural space that fosters creativity and global artistic exchange. The project represents a new chapter in preserving

item.title: A History of Claymation: Key Films and Artists That Defined the Medium
item.description: Claymation, a form of stop-motion animation using clay figures, has captivated audiences for over a century due to its tactile, handmade charm. Beginning with early experiments like The Sculptor’s Welsh Rarebit in 1908, claymation demonstrated that inanimate objects could be brought to life frame by frame. Pioneers like Willis H. O’Brien and Ray Harryhausen elevated the medium, with Harryhausen developing techniques like Dynamation that integrated animated creatures into live-action films. In the U.S., Art Clokey's Gumby introduced a playful and emotionally expressive character style, while Will Vinton coined the term 'Claymation' and brought the medium to mainstream culture through projects such as Closed Mondays, The Adventures of Mark Twain, and the California Raisins. British studio Aardman, led by Nick Park, gave claymation a golden age with Wallace & Gromit, blending humor, warmth, and cinematic precision. Feature films like Chicken Run proved the commercial viability of stop-mot

item.title: Security Footage Released of Alleged Attempted Attack on Trump at Checkpoint
item.description: The news article could not be accessed due to server restrictions, and the full content of the report is unavailable. Based on the headline, the article appears to report on newly released footage showing a person allegedly attempting to breach security to target former President Donald Trump. The footage reportedly captures the individual approaching and confronting a security checkpoint. Authorities may have intervened, and investigations are likely ongoing. The news seems to focus on the threat posed, the security measures in place, and the incident's broader political context, highlighting concerns about the safety of public figures. Without the full article, additional details such as the suspect’s identity, motives, or legal proceedings cannot be confirmed.

item.title: California Governor Faces Budget Challenges Balancing Spending, Taxes, and Legacy
item.description: As California Governor Gavin Newsom approaches the end of his term, he faces critical decisions to address a longstanding structural budget deficit. After years of spending beyond state revenues, Newsom must choose between raising taxes, cutting programs, or relying on temporary financial fixes. While his administration has expanded healthcare for undocumented immigrants, increased child-care support, and provided free school meals, these initiatives, along with the growth of existing programs, have outpaced revenue, leaving the state dependent on reserves and borrowed funds. The Legislative Analyst’s Office warns that the state’s fiscal trajectory is unsustainable without durable solutions, estimating operating deficits exceeding $20 billion annually by 2027-2030. Potential revenue measures under consideration include a billionaire tax initiative, repealing corporate tax breaks, and new fees on large corporations to support Medi-Cal. Newsom, mindful of potential 2028 presidential ambi

item.title: Severe Water Shortages Threaten Corpus Christi and the Colorado River States
item.description: This summer, the United States is facing serious water crises in both local and regional contexts. Corpus Christi, Texas, is approaching a Level 1 drought emergency due to water demand exceeding supply, with projections suggesting municipal water could run out by next year. Surface water sources like Choke Canyon Reservoir and Lake Corpus Christi are critically low, and industrial use, particularly from a major plastics plant, heavily strains local resources. Meanwhile, the Colorado River, a vital water source for 40 million people across seven states, faces a historic low in snowpack and record-high temperatures, severely reducing river flow and threatening hydropower generation at Lake Powell and Lake Mead. Ongoing disputes over water allocation under the 1922 Colorado River Compact exacerbate the crisis, with potential legal conflicts looming if upper basin states fail to deliver their required share to lower basin states. Experts emphasize that climate change and delayed policy res

item.title: Advances in Nanoscale Imaging Reveal New Insights into the Human Immune System
item.description: At WIRED Health, immunologist Daniel Davis of Imperial College London highlighted how cutting-edge microscopy is revealing previously invisible nanoscale processes within the human immune system. These observations are transforming our understanding of how immune cells identify and respond to healthy versus diseased cells. His research, conducted in collaboration with Bristol Myers Squibb, examines how immune cells communicate through tiny protrusions and the immunological synapse, which involves protein molecules that trigger immune responses. By experimenting with reengineered antibodies that enhance these cellular signals, Davis's team aims to strengthen the immune system's ability to target cancer cells. While the research is still in early stages, it holds promise for future patient therapies. Davis also emphasized the highly individual nature of immune health, noting that genetic variations play a major role in how people respond to infections, more so than lifestyle factors alon

item.title: Dana White Announces 85,000 Free Tickets for UFC Event Near the White House
item.description: UFC President and CEO Dana White revealed plans for a historic UFC event at the White House, coinciding with President Donald Trump's birthday and the country's 250th anniversary. Dubbed 'UFC Freedom 250,' the event will feature a temporary arena for 4,300 spectators, with the majority of tickets reserved for military personnel. Additionally, White announced that 85,000 free tickets will be made available to the general public, who can watch the fights on large screens set up at the Ellipse, a park just south of the White House. The setup will include stages, music, and interactive experiences for attendees. White emphasized that the event will proceed regardless of rain or snow, with only lightning posing a threat to the schedule. The military will provide hourly weather updates in the week leading up to the event, allowing organizers to adjust timing if needed. White expressed enthusiasm about hosting an outdoor event in Washington, D.C., despite his usual preference for controlled i

item.title: US Attorney Pirro Provides Update on WHCD Shooting Suspect and Investigation
item.description: US Attorney for the District of Columbia, Jeanine Pirro, spoke with CNN’s Jake Tapper regarding the ongoing investigation into the White House Correspondents' Dinner (WHCD) shooting. Pirro described the suspect as 'far from insane,' providing insight into the alleged gunman's motives and potential targets. She emphasized that, despite being a witness to the incident, she will not recuse herself from the case. The discussion highlighted the seriousness of the investigation and the need for a thorough legal process. Pirro's statements aim to clarify public concern about the suspect’s mental state while ensuring transparency in the prosecution. The interview also touched on the broader context of law enforcement and the measures being taken to protect public figures and events. This case continues to draw national attention due to the high-profile nature of the event and the implications for security at major political gatherings.

item.title: Lawyers Request Review of Suspect's Jail Conditions After Trump Dinner Shooting
item.description: The legal team representing the suspect in the shooting incident at a dinner attended by former President Donald Trump has formally requested that authorities reconsider the conditions of his incarceration. According to reports, the lawyers argue that the current detention circumstances amount to excessive punishment and are negatively affecting their client’s well-being. While the suspect remains in custody, the legal representatives have submitted motions asking the court to either modify his confinement or move him to a different facility that meets standard prison conditions. The case has drawn significant public attention due to the involvement of a high-profile political figure, prompting debates about how suspects in cases with media scrutiny are treated in the criminal justice system. Legal experts note that arguments regarding jail conditions are not uncommon, particularly when inmates are placed in protective custody or segregated units. The court will review the motion and a

item.title: Guidelines for Timing Your Mail-In Vote in the California Primary
item.description: California voters are receiving their mail-in ballots for the June 2 primary, with more than 23 million ballots mailed to active registered voters. The gubernatorial race features a wide field of candidates, including Republican Chad Bianco and Democrat Katie Porter, along with elections for seven other statewide offices, congressional and local races, and the Los Angeles mayoral primary. Campaign strategists offer differing advice: Democratic strategist Paul Maslin encourages voters who are certain about their choices to mail their ballots immediately, while Republican strategist Rob Stutzman suggests waiting to see further developments in this dynamic and relatively unknown candidate field. Kim Alexander of the California Voter Foundation advises a middle path, recommending ballots be mailed by May 26 to ensure timely processing or dropped off in person by May 30. In-person voting remains available statewide until 8 p.m. on election day, except in three rural counties that conduct el

item.title: Weekly roundup of global nuclear issues, policy debates, and geopolitical tensions
item.description:
This weekly newsletter compiles a wide range of developments related to nuclear energy, weapons, environmental risks, and global politics. It blends news coverage, opinion pieces, and curated links, offering a critical perspective on nuclear technologies and their broader implications. Among the highlighted topics are ongoing concerns about nuclear safety, particularly at Chernobyl, where infrastructure remains vulnerable decades after the disaster. Funding efforts, including a reported $100 million U.S. contribution to repair the containment structure, underscore the long-term costs and risks associated with nuclear accidents.
The newsletter also emphasizes geopolitical tensions involving nuclear capabilities, especially in relation to Iran, U.S. foreign policy, and the potential for a renewed nuclear arms race. Commentary suggests that recent conflicts may incentivize nuclear proliferation rather than deter it. In parallel, debates over nuclear energy’s economic viability continue,

item.title: AI System RAVEN Confirms Over 100 Exoplanets and Identifies Rare Planetary Types
item.description: Astronomers at the University of Warwick have utilized a new artificial intelligence system called RAVEN to analyze NASA’s TESS mission data, leading to the confirmation of over 100 exoplanets, including 31 newly discovered worlds. By examining observations from more than 2.2 million stars, the team focused on planets with short orbital periods, completing orbits in under 16 days. Among the confirmed planets are ultra-short-period planets that orbit their stars in less than a day, as well as planets located in the so-called 'Neptunian desert,' a region where planets are rare. RAVEN's advanced machine learning capabilities help distinguish genuine planetary signals from false positives caused by binary stars or instrumental noise. Beyond individual discoveries, the researchers analyzed planetary populations, finding that about 9-10% of Sun-like stars host close-in planets and quantifying the rarity of Neptunian desert planets at only 0.08%. The study demonstrates the power of AI in proc

item.title: Xteink X3: A Compact Magnetic E-Reader to Reduce Social Media Use
item.description: The Xteink X3 is a compact, magnetic e-ink reader designed to help users reduce time spent on social media. Compatible with MagSafe, it can attach directly to the back of an iPhone, making it portable and easy to access. At a price of $80, it offers a small 3.7-inch screen capable of holding hundreds of books, making it convenient for reading on the go, whether in line at a coffee shop or during commutes. The device lacks a touch screen and apps, providing a distraction-free reading experience that contrasts with smartphones. While the default firmware can feel clunky, users can install the open-source CrossPoint firmware for improved usability. The X3 uses a magnetic charger instead of USB-C, though it maintains impressive battery life, dropping only a few percentage points after two weeks of consistent use. A compact magnetic case adds protection and additional carrying options. Limitations include incompatibility with library apps like Libby and Amazon Kindle ebooks due to DRM restr

item.title: Spirit Airlines ceases operations following failed financial rescue
item.description: Spirit Airlines, a U.S.-based low-cost airline known for its extensive a la carte fees, announced it would immediately cease all operations after attempts to secure financial assistance failed. The airline had previously undergone two bankruptcies and had explored the possibility of a White House intervention to prevent closure. Despite being recognized for pioneering extreme low-cost travel, where customers pay extra for services like checked baggage and in-flight refreshments, Spirit could not secure a last-minute lifeline. The shutdown marks the end of a company that had become a notable player in the budget travel sector but struggled to sustain itself financially in a competitive airline market. Industry analysts note that Spirit's business model, while innovative in terms of low fares and optional fees, faced ongoing operational and financial challenges, which ultimately contributed to its closure. The failure of the proposed bailout plan highlights broader concerns about governm

item.title: SIS Plans ₹20,000 Crore Revenue in FY27, Focuses on Market Expansion and Technology-Driven Solutions
item.description: SIS Ltd., an Indian multinational operating in security solutions, facility management, and cash logistics, aims to achieve ₹20,000 crore in revenue for FY27, following a record revenue of approximately ₹16,000 crore in FY26. Group Managing Director Rituraj Sinha outlined that the company targets 15% growth and a 15% return on capital and equity, aligning with its long-term compounding growth strategy. The Indian business, contributing roughly ₹850 crore monthly, is expected to grow over 15%, while international operations maintain an 10–11% annual growth trajectory. SIS’s focus for FY27 will be on expanding market share and moving from service provision to technology-driven solutions, incorporating artificial intelligence (AI), robotics, and drones alongside human labor to enhance efficiency and value. The company currently employs 350,000 personnel, with AI and robotics serving as supportive technologies rather than replacements. Additionally, the new labor codes are seen as benefici

item.title: Why Syphilis Cases in Expectant Mothers Are Rising After the Pandemic
item.description: The rate of syphilis cases in expectant mothers has sharply increased since the COVID-19 pandemic, with a 28% rise in maternal syphilis cases from 2022 to 2024, according to the CDC. Several factors are contributing to this trend, including rising syphilis cases in the general population and barriers to healthcare access for pregnant women. Public health funding for sexually transmitted infections (STIs) has stagnated for decades, and the pandemic worsened healthcare accessibility, particularly for marginalized groups. Social stigma, especially around sexual behavior, also complicates efforts to screen and treat pregnant women for syphilis. Additionally, issues like insurance delays and the difficulty of accessing prenatal care contribute to the rise in congenital syphilis, which can cause severe developmental issues or even stillbirth in infants. Although syphilis is treatable with antibiotics, its prevention relies heavily on timely screenings, which remain a challenge due to gaps in

item.title: Russell Attributes Miami GP Qualifying Deficit to Driving Style, While Antonelli Takes Pole
item.description: George Russell struggled during Miami Grand Prix qualifying, finishing fifth while his Mercedes teammate, Kimi Antonelli, secured pole position by nearly four-tenths of a second. Russell explained that his traditional 'smooth' and precise driving style does not adapt well to low-grip circuits like Miami, Zandvoort, and Brazil. He described the track conditions as challenging, with high temperatures, elevated tire pressures, and low grip making it difficult to find the ideal car balance. Despite this, Russell remains focused on maximizing race results rather than limiting damage. Antonelli, 19, has consistently outperformed Russell in recent events, including Sprint Qualifying, although he received a penalty in the Miami Sprint for exceeding track limits. Analysts suggest Russell may be frustrated by repeatedly trailing his less experienced teammate, but emphasize that he brings consistency and the potential to capitalize on longer-term championship opportunities. Antonelli expressed sa

item.title: New Therapy Could Help Repair Osteoarthritis Damage with a Simple Injection
item.description: Osteoarthritis, a common joint disorder affecting millions worldwide, has traditionally had no cure. However, new breakthroughs in regenerative medicine offer hope. Researchers at the University of Colorado Boulder, funded by a $33.5 million grant from ARPA-H, are working on an experimental treatment that could reverse osteoarthritis damage with a single injection. Instead of prosthetic joints or invasive surgeries, the approach harnesses the body's natural regenerative capabilities. The treatment involves an injection of an approved drug that helps stimulate the body's repair processes by targeting the affected joint. The injection uses a controlled particle system that slowly releases the drug, promoting tissue repair over several months. For more advanced osteoarthritis cases, a separate method uses biomaterials to create a scaffold within the joint, attracting cells to regenerate damaged cartilage and bone. Early animal studies have shown promising results, with joints returning to

item.title: Key Focus Next Week: U.S. Jobs Data and Berkshire Hathaway’s Annual Shareholder Meeting
item.description: The Wall Street Journal live‑coverage card highlights the main events investors should watch over the coming week, centering on the upcoming U.S. nonfarm payrolls report and Berkshire Hathaway’s annual shareholder meeting. The jobs data will help shape expectations for the Federal Reserve’s interest‑rate path, particularly whether the labor market remains strong enough to delay or slow any planned rate cuts. Against that backdrop, stocks across the S&P 500 and Nasdaq have recently reached new highs, bolstered by solid earnings and resilient tech‑sector performance, even as geopolitical tensions and elevated oil prices continue to loom in the background. At the same time, attention is turning to Berkshire Hathaway’s Omaha gathering, where Greg Abel presides as CEO for the first time while Warren Buffett remains on stage. Shareholders will focus on how the conglomerate plans to deploy its record‑sized cash pile, how succession and strategy discussions are framed, and whether the company

item.title: Wealthfront Advisers LLC Invests $174 Million in Biopharmaceutical Company Insmed, Inc.
item.description: Wealthfront Advisers LLC, a well-known investment advisory firm, has recently made a significant move in the financial markets by acquiring a $174 million position in Insmed, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company listed under the ticker INSM. This investment reflects Wealthfront's confidence in Insmed's potential for growth and its innovative drug development pipeline. Insmed specializes in developing therapies for rare diseases, and its recent clinical and regulatory progress may have contributed to attracting institutional investors like Wealthfront. The acquisition of such a substantial stake indicates that the advisory firm sees long-term value in Insmed's strategic direction and product offerings. Market analysts will likely monitor the performance of Insmed’s stock closely following this investment, as large institutional investments often influence market perception and investor sentiment. While the broader market continues to navigate volatility and emerging opportunities in the bi

item.title: Evaluating the Health and Skin Effects of Mosquito Repellent Creams
item.description: Mosquito repellent creams are widely used to prevent mosquito bites and protect against diseases such as dengue, malaria, and chikungunya. These products typically contain chemicals like DEET, picaridin, or natural essential oils designed to repel mosquitoes. While they are effective in reducing the risk of mosquito-borne illnesses, concerns have arisen regarding their potential side effects on the skin. Some users report irritation, redness, or allergic reactions, especially when the creams are applied frequently or in large amounts. Experts recommend selecting products suitable for your skin type, following the application instructions carefully, and avoiding overuse. Additionally, choosing formulations with lower concentrations of active ingredients can help reduce the likelihood of adverse effects. Despite these concerns, the health benefits of preventing mosquito bites generally outweigh the minor skin-related risks for most people. It is also important to consider other preventiv

item.title: The Unregulated World of Health Supplements: Are They Necessary?
item.description: Health supplements have become a booming industry, offering everything from vitamins to weight loss aids. However, with minimal regulation, it's difficult to determine which supplements are beneficial and which are simply gimmicks. While many promise significant health benefits, some are based on questionable science or marketing hype. Experts argue that a balanced diet should be the primary source of nutrients, and supplements should only be used when necessary to fill gaps. Consumers are urged to approach supplements with caution, relying on trusted sources and considering the risks involved in taking products that haven't been rigorously tested. Despite this, the health supplement industry continues to thrive, drawing in millions of dollars annually. This article explores the complexities of the supplement world, the need for better regulation, and whether these products are truly essential or just another trend.

item.title: Top Cash-Back Credit Cards for Everyday Spending
item.description: CNN Underscored highlights some of the best cash-back credit cards available in 2026, focusing on simplicity, rewards potential, and the types of shoppers who can benefit most. Cash-back cards are popular for their straightforward rewards: you earn a percentage back on every dollar spent, which can be redeemed as statement credits, direct deposits, or checks. The article reviews nine top cards, including the Citi Double Cash® Card for its flat 2% cash back on all purchases and no annual fee, and the Blue Cash Preferred® Card from American Express, which offers exceptional rewards on groceries, streaming, gas, and transit, but with a $95 annual fee after the first year. Other cards like the Chase Freedom Flex® provide rotating bonus categories for extra rewards each quarter, while the Citi Custom Cash℠ Card automatically applies 5% cash back to the category where you spend the most, up to $500 per month. The Chase Freedom Unlimited® excels at dining and drugstore purchases, with 1.5% ba

item.title: Camp Mystic Suspends 2026 Summer Season After Tragic Flooding and Legal Struggles
item.description: Camp Mystic, a beloved summer camp in Hunt, Texas, has been a family-run institution for over a century. The camp, which has hosted generations of girls from prominent families, has faced an unimaginable tragedy. In July 2025, flash floods struck the camp, killing 28 people, including 25 campers, two counselors, and camp director Dick Eastland. The devastation has led to lawsuits, investigations, and intense scrutiny over the camp's safety protocols. Despite attempts by the Eastland family to continue the camp’s legacy, the family announced they will not seek a new license for the 2026 summer season. The decision marks the first time in decades that Camp Mystic will remain closed for the summer. This decision comes amid ongoing grief from the tragedy, ongoing lawsuits, and a criminal investigation. The camp’s leadership and history have been intertwined with Texas’ elite families, making this closure a significant blow to the local community. While the Eastlands have expressed their in

item.title: A Curated List of Stylish and Comfortable Fashion Items Available on Amazon
item.description: This article highlights a curated selection of 33 fashion items available on Amazon, emphasizing both style and comfort. The list includes versatile clothing pieces such as ruched tops, matching sets, flowy skirts, rompers, and high-waisted pants suitable for various occasions, from casual home wear to social outings. Accessories like woven totes, earrings, scarves, and handbags are featured for their aesthetic appeal and practicality. Shoes, including kitten heels, ballerina flats, and sandals, are noted for their comfort and stylish designs. The article provides detailed descriptions, sizing information, and price ranges for each item, along with reviews from verified customers praising quality, fit, and versatility. Many of the items are highlighted for their ability to combine comfort with fashion-forward looks, suitable for travel, work, or leisure. Overall, the piece serves as a practical guide for readers seeking affordable, trendy, and wearable fashion directly from Amazon, off

item.title: Global Demand for Chinese Renewable Technologies Spikes Amid Oil Supply Disruptions
item.description: Following the outbreak of the war in Iran, countries facing oil shortages have increasingly turned to renewable energy solutions, with China emerging as a key global supplier. According to the energy think tank Ember, Chinese exports of solar technology, batteries, and electric vehicles (EVs) reached unprecedented levels in March 2026. Specifically, China exported 68 gigawatts of solar technology, a 50% increase over the previous record set in August 2025. Fifty countries, especially in Asia and Africa, recorded their highest-ever imports of Chinese solar products, driven by the ongoing energy crisis. The surge in exports of batteries and EVs also contributed significantly to this growth, rising 70% year-over-year. Chinese battery exports alone reached $10 billion in March, with strong demand from the European Union, Australia, and India. Analysts note that the uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz and the resulting energy security concerns have accelerated the transition to clean ener

item.title: GameStop Builds Stake in eBay Ahead of Potential Acquisition Bid
item.description: Shares of eBay surged approximately 10% in after-hours trading on Friday, May 1, 2026, following a Wall Street Journal report that GameStop has been quietly accumulating a stake in the online resale platform in preparation for a potential acquisition offer. The video game retailer, led by CEO Ryan Cohen, could submit a bid as early as later this month, according to sources familiar with the matter. GameStop's shares also climbed around 8% in after-hours trading, reflecting investor enthusiasm for the ambitious move. This development aligns with Cohen's strategy to transform GameStop into a major e-commerce powerhouse valued at over $100 billion. eBay, with a market capitalization of about $46 billion, dwarfs GameStop's roughly $12 billion valuation, posing significant financing and integration challenges for any deal. During regular trading, eBay closed up 0.6% at $104.07, up 19% year-to-date, while GameStop ended 6.3% higher at $26.53, gaining 32% so far in 2026. The report highlights

item.title: Gavin Newsom Explains His Social Media Criticism of Trump and Declines to Confirm 2028 Presidential Bid
item.description: During a Friday appearance on HBO's “Real Time with Bill Maher,” California Governor Gavin Newsom addressed his controversial social media activity targeting former President Donald Trump. Maher noted that Newsom’s approach resembled Trump’s own tactics, including suing Fox News for defamation. Newsom countered by saying he is not imitating Trump but rather holding up a “mirror” to highlight what he sees as extreme or abnormal behavior. He cited examples such as Trump cosplaying as religious figures, placing his image on Mount Rushmore, and using all-caps messaging online. Newsom argued that his actions aim to call out a pattern of behavior that the media often overlooks. Throughout the interview, Maher attempted to discuss Newsom’s potential 2028 presidential candidacy, but the governor consistently avoided a direct answer, redirecting the conversation back to Trump’s conduct. While polls reportedly show Newsom as a frontrunner for the Democratic nomination, he refrained from committi

item.title: Digital Artist Paulo de Almeida Transforms National Flags into Nature Landscapes
item.description: In a creative digital art series titled Flags of Nature, artist Paulo de Almeida reimagines familiar national flags as living, three-dimensional environments drawn from the natural world. Using photo-realistic 3D rendering, the artworks replace the flat colored blocks of traditional flags with elements such as snow-capped mountains, dense forests, flowing leaves, and sweeping landscapes that echo each country's symbolic colors and patterns. The concept blends graphic design identity with atmospheric natural scenery, making the flags feel tactile, organic, and alive rather than purely symbolic. The images evoke a strong sense of place and texture, blending digital precision with a soft, realistic quality that avoids feeling overly artificial. According to the article on Moss and Fog, while the series offers compelling glimpses of this idea, it feels somewhat incomplete, serving more as an intriguing preview of a larger potential project. The work highlights the intersection of digital a

item.title: President Trump Shares 11 Posts on Truth Social During 42-Minute Late-Night Session
item.description: On Friday night, May 2, 2026, President Donald Trump engaged in a rapid posting session on Truth Social, sharing 11 posts within a 42-minute window between 11:03 p.m. and 11:45 p.m. The content ranged from personal and family images to political commentary and visual edits promoting his vision for Washington, D.C. landmarks. Among the posts were an AI-generated image of Trump relaxing in the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool with Vice President JD Vance and other officials, a photo of First Lady Melania Trump with White House staff, and an image of Rep. Hakeem Jeffries holding a baseball bat that Trump captioned critically, calling him a 'THUG' and a danger to the country. Trump also shared an altered image of himself holding Uno 'Wild' cards with the text 'I HAVE ALL THE CARDS,' along with artistic illustrations of his face, including one superimposed on Mount Rushmore. Several posts focused on the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool renovation, contrasting images labeled with 'Hussein Ob

item.title: Boston Blue Episode 17 Recap: Erin Returns and Lena Discovers Family Secrets
item.description: In Season 1, Episode 17 of 'Boston Blue,' titled 'L'dor Vador,' multiple storylines unfold that impact the Reagan and Silver families. Danny (Donnie Wahlberg) escorts his sister Erin (Bridget Moynahan) back to New York for a Reagan family dinner, though the event occurs off-screen. Before that, the siblings successfully apprehend a serial killer and rescue his latest victim. Tensions arise as not everyone supports Danny’s move to Massachusetts, with Abraham attempting to provoke both siblings and exposing Erin’s mixed feelings. Erin later clarifies to Danny that she isn’t upset about his relocation, though her honesty is somewhat questionable. Meanwhile, Lena learns the truth about her biological father, Chris Williams (Erik King), discovering that he has turned his life around after past troubles and is now a small-town police chief. During her search, Lena also uncovers that Chris has a second daughter, Christina (Alisha Wainwright), revealing her as Lena’s half-sister. These revelat

item.title: Some European Countries Consider Suspending EES System to Ease Airport Crowds
item.description: Following Greece's recent decision to suspend the EU Entry/Exit System (EES) for Serbian citizens from June 1 to August 30, news indicates that Italy and Portugal are considering similar measures for tourists to avoid overcrowding at their borders. The EES requires fingerprint registration and photographs of travelers from outside the EU, including countries like Serbia and the UK, to enter the Schengen Area. Travelers have reported significant delays, with incidents such as over 100 easyJet passengers missing flights in Milan last month, and tourists during early May holidays experiencing hours-long passport checks in Bergamo. Airports in Portugal are reportedly allowing passengers through if lines become excessively long, and Italy may follow Greece's example by permitting entry with a passport stamp. Travel experts suggest that Spain, France, and Croatia could adopt the same approach if the system continues to cause disruptions. Seamus McCauley from Holiday Extras highlighted that t

item.title: Melty Blood: Twi-Lumina Coming to Switch 1 & 2 in Early 2027
item.description: At EVO Japan, developer French Bread and publisher Aniplex unveiled the upcoming 2D fighting game Melty Blood: Twi-Lumina with a teaser trailer. Scheduled for release in early 2027, the game will be available on both the Nintendo Switch and the Switch 2. Melty Blood: Twi-Lumina is described as a return of Melty Blood: Type Lumina, promising refined battle mechanics, new characters, and a continuation of the story set within the Tsukihime universe. The original Type Lumina was released on the Switch eShop in 2021 and has since received multiple DLC updates and a compatibility fix for the newer Switch model. Fans have expressed excitement over the announcement, with discussions around potential guest characters from other Type-Moon titles and hopes for features like crossplay and improved rollback netcode. While specifics about the new roster and gameplay adjustments are still under wraps, the teaser hints at returning characters and additional content aligned with the Tsukihime remake u

item.title: AI Struggles Again to Predict Kentucky Derby Winners
